steps.title: Set up tangent
steps.content:
For the right triangle formed by the ramp, tangent is opposite over adjacent. The height is $29.1$, and the horizontal distance is $x$, so
$$\tan t=\frac{29.1}{x}$$

steps.title: Use the boundary value
steps.content:
The law requires the tangent value to be less than $$\frac{1}{12}$$ The least possible horizontal distance is found from the boundary equation.
$$\frac{29.1}{x}=\frac{1}{12}$$

steps.title: Solve for the distance
steps.content: Cross-multiply to find the boundary distance. $$x=29.1(12)=349.2$$ Distances greater than this satisfy the strict inequality, so the least boundary value is the requested result. The answer is 349.2.
